extracted_information: The web content provides the OWASP Top 10 list for 2021, and announces the upcoming release of the OWASP Top 10:2025 in late summer/early fall 2025. It does not contain information specific to OWASP Top 10 security vulnerabilities for 2024. General application security best practices are briefly mentioned, primarily advocating for the adoption of the OWASP Top 10 and concepts like threat modeling and secure design. Specific security testing methodologies or automated security testing approaches are not detailed.

owasp_top_10_2021.name: Broken Access Control
owasp_top_10_2021.description: Moved up from fifth position; 94% of applications tested for some form. 34 CWEs mapped to it had more occurrences than any other category.

owasp_top_10_2021.name: Cryptographic Failures
owasp_top_10_2021.description: Shifted up to #2, previously 'Sensitive Data Exposure'. Focuses on failures related to cryptography leading to sensitive data exposure or system compromise.

owasp_top_10_2021.name: Injection
owasp_top_10_2021.description: Slides down to #3. 94% of applications tested for some form. 33 CWEs mapped to this category have second most occurrences. Cross-site Scripting is now included.

owasp_top_10_2021.name: Insecure Design
owasp_top_10_2021.description: New category for 2021, focusing on risks from design flaws. Calls for threat modeling, secure design patterns, principles, and reference architectures.

owasp_top_10_2021.name: Security Misconfiguration
owasp_top_10_2021.description: Moves up from #6. 90% of applications tested for some form. Former XML External Entities (XXE) is now part of this category.

owasp_top_10_2021.name: Vulnerable and Outdated Components
owasp_top_10_2021.description: Previously 'Using Components with Known Vulnerabilities'. #2 in community survey, sufficient data for Top 10. Moves up from #9 in 2017. Only category without mapped CVEs; default exploit/impact weights of 5.0 factored.

owasp_top_10_2021.name: Identification and Authentication Failures
owasp_top_10_2021.description: Previously 'Broken Authentication'. Sliding down from #2. Now includes CWEs related to identification failures. Increased availability of standardized frameworks seems to be helping.

owasp_top_10_2021.name: Software and Data Integrity Failures
owasp_top_10_2021.description: New category for 2021. Focuses on assumptions related to software updates, critical data, CI/CD pipelines without integrity verification. Insecure Deserialization from 2017 is now part of this category. High weighted impact from CVE/CVSS data for its 10 mapped CWEs.

owasp_top_10_2021.name: Security Logging and Monitoring Failures
owasp_top_10_2021.description: Previously 'Insufficient Logging & Monitoring'. Added from industry survey (#3), moved up from #10 previously. Expanded to include more failure types. Challenging to test, not well represented in CVE/CVSS data. Failures impact visibility, incident alerting, forensics.

owasp_top_10_2021.name: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
owasp_top_10_2021.description: Added from Top 10 community survey (#1). Relatively low incidence rate with above average testing coverage, and above-average ratings for Exploit and Impact potential. Community feedback indicates importance despite current data.
